title: Sort Formula Date Columns Correctly
excerpt: I really want to be able to sort a formula column that has a date. For example I have a formula column that picks the latest of two dates after today, but if I try and sort that column earliest to latest it doesn’t curre…

posts.author: huwrc (Huw)
posts.cookedHtml: I really want to be able to sort a formula column that has a date. For example I have a formula column that picks the latest of two dates after today, but if I try and sort that column earliest to latest it doesn’t currently recognise the results as a date

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @huwrc - you could use General Caster to calculate the formula and cast the result to a Date column, then you can sort on it.

Thanks,
Mark

Green Llamas

monday.com + Green Llamas

Green Llamas is here to help you make all your People and Projects Green. Let us get your herds under control using the monday.com platform.

posts.author: ChristineWLF (Christine)
posts.cookedHtml: I use a formula column to determine a target date. I’d like to sort my board by that column soonest due but currently it only sorts month date NOT year. So the 2023, 2024 & 2025 are all mixed together.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Christine!

I hear you on this.

I wanted to check if the existing feature requests relates to what you’re hoping to achieve? If so, we can go ahead and merge your request into it to increase votes and put greater emphasis on its development

Let me know!

posts.author: ChristineWLF (Christine)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes I think that is the same request. I liked that one.
